title: Research Engineer — Conversational Agent (Etihad Airways)
description: Built ingestion/cleaning pipelines for Adobe and NICE; extracted personas and planned journeys; unified these into a single retrieval layer for the agent.
Designed structured response schemas (single/multi-flight) with “flight transparency,” and extended planning/LLM logic to support all flight options on request.
Implemented real-time audio capability for the Etihad chatbot using ChatGPT's streaming API, enabling full-duplex voice interactions.
Integrated critical APIs (incl. return-journey), enabled targeted supervised fine-tuning, parallelized conversational state for latency, and added bi-directional audio with the same policies.
Designed and simulated a UserLM to emulate user behavior patterns, supporting training, evaluation, and stress-testing of the conversational system.

title: AdvSim2Real — Adversarial Simulation for Agent Systems
description: Strengthening LLM-agent pipelines against realistic failure modes through dynamic adversarial simulation.
Probing retrieval, memory, tool use, and multi-turn behavior under pressure to surface failure modes that do not appear in static evaluation.

title: UGRIP Mentor — BraTS MICCAI Challenge
description: Mentored 6 UGRIP interns and actively participated in the BraTS MICCAI challenge.
Enhanced the MedNeXt base model for BraTS 2023 by integrating a novel schedule-free optimizer introduced by Meta AI in the paper “The Road Less Scheduled”.
Implemented both greedy and uniform model souping techniques across 18 trained models with varied hyper-parameters to boost performance.
Awarded the Best Team Award within the UGRIP program.
Achieved 3rd place in MICCAI's prestigious BraTS 2024 SSA & Pediatrics Challenge and presented our research at MICCAI 2024.

title: Machine Learning Engineer
description: Developed a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) chatbot system that processes Excel datasets to deliver comprehensive data explanations, interactive QA responses, and actionable analytics insights.
Handled different meetings' requirements and goals with stakeholders.

title: Research Intern — Autonomous Last-Mile Vehicle
description: Developed a Drive-by-Wire system for an E-rickshaw, enhancing autonomous control capabilities.
Calibrated VLP-16 LiDAR and stereo cameras to improve sensor accuracy and reliability.
Utilized Commonroad Scenario Designer to create lanelet maps for IIIT Delhi OSM map and Carla open drive maps — [documentation](https://hackmd.io/9IH3-cV1T5it9JmIgcOSLw).
Implemented a PID controller for throttle and steering control of the E-rickshaw, ensuring smooth and responsive maneuvers.
Trained and deployed YOLOP for real-time object detection and perception tasks — [video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=MVEGYVS_Tus).

title: Research Intern — Linear Friction Actuation
description: Worked on Linear Friction Actuation to enhance mechanical system performance.
Designed an image-processing application to analyze photos of friction stir-processed material samples, extracting valuable data for research purposes.
Automated linear rails using a ball screw, stepper motor, Arduino Uno, and TB6600 motor driver for precise control of a welding torch.
institution: Jamia Millia Islamia · Supervisor: Dr. Arshad Noor Siddique
